evidence: The source receipt found no retained release-candidate directory in the repository inventory.
docs/standards/versioning.md defines private package versioning and future publication conditions, but not an immutable candidate-retention policy.
packages/scripts/README.md documents local packed-artifact validation but not candidate identity, storage, retention, promotion, or recovery.
rejectedAlternatives: Treat a locally packed tarball as a retained, promotable, or published candidate.
Treat a successful local release:check or packed-artifact check as publication proof.
Move or retain package tarballs as release evidence.
acceptedOrDefaultBehavior: Package tarballs are transient local test inputs. The owning command deletes them; they are never retained, promotable, or published candidates. Retain only sanitized commit, package, digest, and check receipts, never the tarballs themselves.

taskBlocking.inheritedNonClaim: Packed-artifact validation does not create an immutable release candidate.
Runbooks must describe transient test inputs and sanitized receipts, not candidate retention or promotion.
Automation may delete its own transient tarballs and retain only approved sanitized receipts.

authorityImpact: The release maintainer accepted the transient-input policy. This record grants no artifact storage, promotion, registry, publication, or deployment authority.
nonClaims: No immutable candidate artifact, promotion route, registry state, or publication exists by virtue of this record.
This record does not create, move, retain, publish, or delete a package tarball.
carryingCost: Owning commands must keep tarball cleanup scoped to their own transient inputs and receipts sanitized.
reviewOrResumeTrigger: Any request to retain, promote, publish, or externally store a tarball requires a superseding decision and separately authorized operation.
